footer.built_by_post: — an AI engineer learning GR by building tools for it.

home.hero_title: Interactive black-hole physics, in your browser.
home.hero_lede: Spacetime Lab is a Python + web platform for exploring exact solutions of General Relativity — from the Schwarzschild horizon to the Page curve of an evaporating black hole.

schwarzschild.subtitle: Phase 1 · v0.1 → v0.4
schwarzschild.desc: The simplest exact black hole. Sliders for mass; live readouts of horizon, ISCO, photon sphere, Hawking T.

kerr.subtitle: Phase 3 · v0.3 → v1.2
kerr.desc: Rotating black hole. Tune (M, a/M); see the ergosphere deform, ISCO split into prograde / retrograde.

btz.subtitle: Phase 8 · v0.8 → v1.3
btz.desc: The 3D AdS black hole — bulk dual of the BTZ Page curve. Strominger-Cardy match verified to machine precision.

rn.subtitle: Phase 4 deferred · v3.1 in UI
rn.desc: Charged static BH. Cosmic censorship enforced; closed-form horizons + photon sphere; ISCO numerical from dL²/dr=0.

kn.subtitle: v3.2 in UI
kn.desc: Rotating + charged BH. Newman et al. 1965 — combines Kerr and RN. All three limits cross-checked bit-exactly.

ds.subtitle: v3.2 partial · cosmology
ds.desc: Λ>0 vacuum, static patch. Cosmological horizon, Gibbons-Hawking radiation. The Λ-only 'easy half' of the FLRW family.

geo.subtitle: Phase 3 · v0.3 (v2.6 in UI)
geo.desc: Integrate timelike geodesics in Kerr or Schwarzschild via the symplectic implicit-midpoint integrator. 3D scene with horizons, ergosphere, animated trail.

penrose.title: Penrose Diagrams
penrose.subtitle: Phase 2 · v0.2 → v1.5
penrose.desc: Maximally extended causal structure. Pan & zoom over Minkowski and Schwarzschild conformal diagrams.

holo.subtitle: Phase 7-9 · v0.7 → v2.1
holo.desc: Page curves for both eternal BTZ and evaporating Schwarzschild — the resolution of the information paradox in two interactive plots.

home.stats_caption: Built sequentially through Phase 1 (Schwarzschild) → Phase 9 (Page curve, v1.0) → v2.x post-release sprint (real QES + replica wormholes + higher-d RT).

schwarzschild.title: Schwarzschild Black Hole
schwarzschild.desc: The simplest exact black hole solution — a non-rotating, uncharged, spherically symmetric vacuum spacetime.

kerr.title: Kerr Black Hole
kerr.desc_pre: The two-parameter rotating vacuum solution. As
kerr.desc_post: grows from 0 (Schwarzschild) toward 1 (extremal), the inner and outer horizons approach, the ergosphere bulges, and the ISCO splits into prograde and retrograde branches.
kerr.what_changes_title: What changes as you spin it up?
kerr.bullet_c: ISCO splits: prograde shrinks down to {{tex}} at extremal (energy efficiency 42%), retrograde grows toward {{tex2}}.
kerr.bullet_d_post: : extremal Kerr is a zero-temperature black hole (third law).
kerr.cite: Reference: Boyer & Lindquist 1967; Bardeen, Press & Teukolsky 1972; Wald, General Relativity, ch. 12.

btz.title: BTZ — 3D AdS Black Hole
btz.desc: The Bañados-Teitelboim-Zanelli black hole: a quotient of AdS₃ that is locally pure AdS but globally a black hole. Its boundary CFT entropy from Cardy's formula reproduces the Bekenstein-Hawking area law (Strominger 1998), the simplest microscopic counting in quantum gravity.
btz.match_p1_pre: For BTZ, the boundary CFT (a 2D CFT with central charge
btz.match_p1_post: from Brown-Henneaux) has a Cardy formula for asymptotic state degeneracy:
btz.match_p2_post_post: . The "Cardy check" card above shows the numerical ratio — should read exactly 1 to machine precision.
btz.cite: Reference: Bañados, Teitelboim, Zanelli 1992; Brown & Henneaux 1986; Strominger 1998. Bit-exact verification across 6 parameter combinations in tests/test_phase8.py.

rn.title: Reissner-Nordström Black Hole
rn.desc_pre: The unique static, spherically symmetric, asymptotically-flat Einstein-Maxwell solution. As
rn.desc_post: grows from 0 (Schwarzschild) toward 1 (extremal), the inner and outer horizons approach, the photon sphere shrinks, and the Hawking temperature drops to zero.
rn.spherical_caption: Spherical slice (any θ, φ) · all surfaces are spheres
rn.what_changes_title: What changes as you charge it up?
rn.bullet_a: At Q = 0: pure Schwarzschild — r₊ = 2M, r₋ = 0, ISCO = 6M, photon sphere = 3M.
rn.bullet_b: For 0 < |Q| < M: r₊ shrinks from 2M toward M; r₋ grows from 0 toward M. Vieta's relations: r₊ + r₋ = 2M, r₊ · r₋ = Q².
rn.bullet_c: ISCO decreases monotonically with |Q|; photon sphere shrinks toward 2M.
rn.bullet_d: At extremality (|Q| = M): r₊ = r₋ = M, T_H = 0 (third law), entropy S = π M².
rn.cite: Reference: Reissner 1916; Nordström 1918; Wald §6.4; MTW §31.5. ISCO formula: Pradhan & Iyer 2010. Bit-exact tests pin all closed-form invariants to ≤ 1e-12 in tests/test_reissner_nordstrom.py.

kn.title: Kerr-Newman Black Hole
kn.desc: The two-charge generalisation of Kerr — the unique stationary, axisymmetric, asymptotically-flat solution of the Einstein-Maxwell equations sourced by a point mass with both spin and electric charge (Newman et al. 1965). Reduces to Kerr at Q = 0, to RN at a = 0, to Schwarzschild at both.
kn.formula_caption_pre: Boyer-Lindquist; the only thing Q changes vs. Kerr is the extra
kn.bullet_schwarzschild: Schwarzschild (a=0, Q=0): r₊ = 2M, no ergosphere, no Cauchy horizon.
kn.bullet_kerr: Kerr (Q=0): the page already exists at /kerr; this page reproduces it bit-exactly when you slide |Q|/M to 0.
kn.bullet_rn: Reissner-Nordström (a=0): the page at /reissner-nordstrom; this page reproduces it bit-exactly when you slide a/M to 0.
kn.bullet_both: Both: r₊ + r₋ = 2M, r₊ r₋ = a² + Q² (3-parameter Vieta's generalisation; "Vieta r₊r₋" card above checks it client-side).
kn.bullet_extremal: Extremal (a² + Q² = M²): horizons merge at r = M, T_H = 0 (third law).
kn.cite_paper: Reference: Newman et al., J. Math. Phys. 6, 918 (1965) — "Metric of a rotating, charged mass". Wald §12.3. Bit-exact tests pin all three limits in tests/test_kerr_newman.py.
kn.cite_isco: ISCO closed form (Aliev-Galtsov 1981) deferred to v3.2.1 — the polynomial is genuinely gnarly. Kerr's ISCO endpoints (/api/metrics/kerr) cover the Q=0 slice, and RN's (/api/metrics/reissner-nordstrom) cover the a=0 slice.

desitter.title: de Sitter spacetime
desitter.desc_pre: The unique maximally-symmetric vacuum solution of Einstein with positive cosmological constant
desitter.desc_mid1: . Static patch coordinates show a *cosmological* event horizon at
desitter.desc_mid2: — analogous in form to Schwarzschild's BH horizon, but the metric is regular at the centre and the horizon is observer-dependent (each observer's past light cone has its own).
desitter.radius_hint_post: — three equivalent ways to think about it
desitter.bullet_curvature_post: everywhere; both the Ricci tensor and Riemann tensor are proportional to combinations of g_{μν}.
desitter.bullet_horizon_pre: Cosmological horizon: every observer sees a horizon at proper distance
desitter.bullet_horizon_post: . It is NOT a BH — there's no singularity inside, no ergosphere, no ISCO.
desitter.bullet_gh_pre: Gibbons-Hawking radiation (1977): the horizon emits thermal radiation at
desitter.bullet_gh_post: — the cosmological analogue of Hawking radiation from a BH horizon.
desitter.bullet_entropy_pre: Bekenstein-Hawking entropy of a horizon you don't own:
desitter.bullet_entropy_post: . Holds even though no matter has fallen across this horizon — entropy from observer-dependent ignorance.
desitter.bullet_flrw_post: : an FLRW universe dominated by Λ asymptotes to dS. Our late-ΛCDM universe is approximately dS.
desitter.cite_paper: Reference: Wald §5.2; Hawking & Ellis §5.2; Gibbons & Hawking, Phys. Rev. D 15, 2738 (1977). Bit-exact tests pin all closed-form invariants to ≤ 1e-12 in tests/test_de_sitter.py.
desitter.cite_flrw: General FLRW (with arbitrary equation of state, mixed matter components, Friedmann ODE) deferred to v3.2.full — see docs/v3.2-flrw-de-sitter-spec.md. This page covers the closed-form Λ-only "easy half".

geodesics.title: Geodesic Explorer
geodesics.lede: Integrate timelike or null geodesics in Kerr / Schwarzschild via the symplectic implicit-midpoint integrator (Phase 3, v0.3). Click on the equatorial disk in the 3D scene below to set a new initial position, or use the preset library.
geodesics.btn_add_tooltip: Add a new trajectory alongside the existing ones
geodesics.scene_title: 3D scene · click on the dark disk to set r₀, φ₀

stable_inclined.name: Stable inclined orbit
stable_inclined.desc: Kerr a=0.5M, r=10M, prograde, slight inclination

plunge.name: Plunge to horizon
plunge.desc: Kerr a=0.5M, starts at r=10M with strong inward p_r — falls in

schw_bound.name: Schwarzschild bound orbit
schw_bound.desc: Schwarzschild M=1, eccentric bound orbit at r=8M

kerr_inclined.name: Highly inclined Kerr
kerr_inclined.desc: Kerr a=0.9M, large p_θ — strong out-of-plane motion

photon.name: Photon deflection (null)
photon.desc: Schwarzschild M=1, b≈4.5 < b_c=3√3 ≈ 5.20 — photon swings past BH

penrose.title: Penrose Diagrams
penrose.lede_pre: Conformal compactifications that bring the entire causal structure of an infinite spacetime onto a finite diagram. Light rays are exactly 45° lines; future and past timelike infinity (
penrose.mass_hint: Mass affects the labelled horizon position; the conformal shape itself is mass-independent.
penrose.overlay_label_pre: Overlay a Schwarzschild bound geodesic (region I) — projected via
penrose.bullet_a: Light rays travel at 45° — that's the whole point of the conformal compactification: it preserves angles.
penrose.bullet_b: Move up in the diagram = move forward in time. Past infinity at the bottom, future at the top.
penrose.bullet_c: For Schwarzschild: regions I (our universe), II (BH interior), III (other universe), IV (white hole). No causal contact between I and III.
penrose.bullet_d: The two diagonal lines inside region II are the future event horizons. Once crossed, you can only end at the singularity (the horizontal jagged line).
penrose.cite: Reference: Penrose 1964 (causal structure); Kruskal 1960, Szekeres 1960 (Schwarzschild maximal extension); Hawking & Ellis, The Large Scale Structure of Space-Time, ch. 5. SVG renderer shipped in v1.5.0.

holography.title: Holography & the Page Curve
holography.lede: The shape of the Hawking radiation entropy as a black hole evaporates. Don Page predicted in 1993 that, if quantum gravity is unitary, this curve must turn around — first growing (radiation looks thermal), then shrinking back (radiation purifies the black hole). The curve below is the modern derivation via the island formula.

eternal.title: Eternal BTZ — Hartman-Maldacena vs. island saddle
eternal.explainer_pre: Linear-growth phase from the Hartman-Maldacena saddle dominates early; the constant island saddle takes over once it equals
eternal.explainer_mid: . The crossing time is the Page time

evaporating.title: Evaporating Schwarzschild — bell-shaped Page curve
evaporating.explainer_post: ≈ 0.6464 t_evap. The bell shape returns to zero — toy-model unitarity for an evaporating black hole.

conservation.title: Conservation diagnostics
conservation.legend_post: — small drift is the diagnostic that the trajectory follows true geodesics.
